source.language_note: Rulebook is Korean; numerics reproduced in SI.
source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the Korea Drone Soccer 2025 ball architecture (inner quadcopter frame + outer geodesic cage + cage-to-frame spokes/mounting rings). No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the spherical protective cage ('ball') that surrounds a Korea Drone Soccer Association 2025 drone as a multi-part assembly of an inner quadcopter frame + outer geodesic polycarbonate/CFRP cage + cage-to-frame mounting rings. The cage is the defining structural element that lets the drone collide with opponents, walls, and goal rings without damaging the inner quadrotor. Per the 2025 Korean rulebook, the cage outer diameter must be 400 mm +/- 20 mm (i.e., 380 to 420 mm). Inside the cage a standard 150 mm quadrotor is mounted via a single central hub. The cage must pass cleanly through a goal inner diameter of 600 mm mounted at 3.0 to 3.5 m height. Geometry: the cage is a geodesic icosahedral framework with 30 struts (edges of an icosahedron) connecting 12 vertex nodes; the struts are 3 mm outer diameter carbon-fibre pultruded tubes with 0.5 mm wall (2 mm inner diameter); the nodes are 3D-printed nylon 6-carbon-fibre (nylon-CF) spherical junctions, 10 mm outer diameter. A central hub 80 mm diameter, 20 mm thick, made of 3D-printed nylon-CF, is suspended by three equally-spaced 60 mm long 2 mm OD, 1.5 mm ID carbon tube spokes to three cage nodes on one great-circle. Total drone + cage mass must remain below 1000 g (standard class). Material for struts: pultruded CFRP (Ex = 130 GPa, Ey = 9 GPa, density 1500 kg/m^3, tensile allowable 1500 MPa, compressive 900 MPa). Material for nodes and hub: nylon 6-CF (E = 8.5 GPa, yield ~85 MPa, density 1150 kg/m^3). Load cases: LC1 wall collision at 8 m/s, 30 mm contact patch; LC2 opponent collision 8 m/s closing; LC3 forward-flight drag 1 N; LC4 hub thrust reaction per spoke 5.87 N; LC5 1.5 m ground drop. Pass/fail: (R1a) LC1/LC2 strut stress <= 600 MPa; (R1b) LC1/LC2 node stress <= 55 MPa; (R2) LC1 linear buckling >= 2.0; (R3) LC4 spoke stress <= 600 MPa; (R4) LC5 residual cage diameter change <= 5 mm; (R5) cage+hub+spokes mass <= 250 g; (R6) outer envelope under any LC <= 420 mm. Primary verification: explicit dynamic FEA for LC1, LC2, LC5; linear static for LC3, LC4; linear buckling for LC1 peak. Vibration modal, thermal, fluid, radiation not required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly + report.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The ball is decomposed into an INNER quadcopter frame + OUTER cage + CAGE-TO-FRAME interface. Inner: (A) 80 mm diameter nylon-CF center plate/hub (20 mm thick, carries flight controller + battery + receiver); (B) 4 quadrotor arms (CFRP tubes 80 mm long, 3 mm OD, 0.5 mm wall) radiating from the center plate; (C) 4 motor mount pads at the arm tips. Outer: (D) 30 geodesic CFRP struts forming the icosahedral edges (all 3 mm OD x 0.5 mm wall); (E) 12 nylon-CF icosahedral vertex nodes bonding the struts. Cage-to-frame: (F) 2 cage-to-frame mounting rings (one upper, one lower) each connected to 3 cage nodes on a great-circle and to the center plate via three 60 mm spoke tubes (6 spokes total); (G) fastener / adhesive family (structural adhesive at node-strut + screw clamps at ring interfaces). Interfaces: IF1 struts bonded into nodes (all 30 x 2 = 60 strut-node bonds); IF2 spokes bonded into nodes (6); IF3 spokes bolted/bonded to cage-to-frame mounting rings (6); IF4 mounting rings bolted to inner center plate (2 x 4 M2 screws); IF5 arms bonded into center plate (4); IF6 motor pads bolted/bonded to arm tips (4). FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED multi-body model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 wall collision peak). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the spherical protective cage ('ball') surrounding a Korea Drone Soccer Association 2025 drone as a multi-part assembly of inner quadcopter frame + outer geodesic polycarbonate/CFRP cage + cage-to-frame mounting rings.

Envelope: cage outer diameter 400 mm +/- 20 mm (380 to 420 mm); inner 150 mm quadrotor on a central hub; must pass a 600 mm goal at 3.0 to 3.5 m height. Icosahedral framework: 30 struts (3 mm OD pultruded CFRP, 0.5 mm wall), 12 nylon-CF vertex nodes (10 mm OD). Central hub 80 mm diameter x 20 mm thick on three 60 mm spoke tubes. Total drone + cage mass <= 1000 g (standard class).

Materials: struts pultruded CFRP (tensile 1500 MPa, compressive 900 MPa); nodes/hub nylon-CF (yield ~85 MPa).

Load cases: LC1 wall collision at 8 m/s on 30 mm contact patch; LC2 opponent collision 8 m/s closing; LC3 forward-flight drag 1 N; LC4 hub thrust reaction 5.87 N per spoke; LC5 1.5 m ground drop.

Pass/fail: (R1a) strut LC1/LC2 <= 600 MPa; (R1b) node LC1/LC2 <= 55 MPa; (R2) LC1 buckling >= 2.0; (R3) LC4 spoke <= 600 MPa; (R4) LC5 residual diameter change <= 5 mm; (R5) cage+hub+spokes mass <= 250 g; (R6) envelope <= 420 mm.

prompt.title: Korea Drone Soccer ball (multi-part inner quadcopter + outer geodesic cage assembly)
prompt.brief: Drone Soccer 400 mm ball: inner 80 mm center plate + 4 arms + 4 motor pads (quadcopter); outer icosahedral cage of 30 CFRP struts + 12 nylon-CF vertex nodes; 2 cage-to-frame mounting rings joined by 6 spokes to the center plate; fastener/adhesive family. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under LC1 governing 8 m/s wall collision.

boundary_conditions.mounting: free-free for explicit (LC1, LC2, LC5); inner center plate constrained for LC3/LC4
boundary_conditions.loads: contact force at cage vertex, drag at cage forward face, or thrust reaction at spokes

parts.name: inner_center_plate
parts.role: nylon-CF center plate / hub carrying flight controller, battery, receiver (80 mm OD x 20 mm)

parts.name: quadcopter_arm
parts.role: CFRP arm from center plate to motor pad (4 x)

parts.name: motor_mount_pad
parts.role: motor mount pad at each arm tip (4 x, typically printed nylon-CF)

parts.name: cage_strut
parts.role: geodesic icosahedral CFRP strut edge (30 total)

parts.name: cage_vertex_node
parts.role: nylon-CF spherical vertex joint bonding the 5 meeting struts (12 total)

parts.name: cage_to_frame_mounting_ring
parts.role: upper and lower great-circle mounting rings each bonding to 3 cage nodes and linking to the center plate via 3 spokes

parts.name: cage_to_frame_spoke
parts.role: thin CFRP spoke tube linking mounting ring to center plate (6 total, 3 per ring)

parts.name: fastener_adhesive_set
parts.role: structural adhesive at node-strut + spoke-ring bonds; M2 screws at ring-to-center-plate and motor-pad interfaces

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 400 mm diameter sphere, cage passes through 600 mm goal ring
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: spherical centered on center plate; cage icosahedron aligned with orthotropic axes

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC1 8 m/s wall collision peak).

interfaces.load_path: collision strut compression/tension into vertex node (60 strut-node bonds total)
spoke load into 3 cage-node interface on great-circle
spoke load into cage-to-frame mounting ring
quadcopter thrust reaction + LC1 collision momentum exchange between inner frame and outer cage
quadrotor arm thrust reaction into center plate
motor thrust at arm tip into motor mount pad

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. The LC1 8 m/s wall collision excites both the outer cage (strut-node bonds, great-circle deformation) and the inner quadcopter (through the 6 spokes into the mounting rings); assembly-level DCR is required, per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A Korea Drone Soccer ball is by construction an assembly of an outer icosahedral cage (30 struts + 12 nodes) surrounding an inner quadcopter (center plate + arms + motor pads), joined by mounting rings and spokes; collisions and thrust both cross bonded and bolted interfaces that must be modelled as a multi-body assembly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Spoke count (6 = 2 rings x 3), mounting-ring count (2), and inner quadcopter arm/motor-pad inclusion are programmatic speculation consistent with the rulebook 400 mm cage + 150 mm quadrotor topology; strut/node counts, loads, materials, and pass/fail thresholds inherit the original brief verbatim.
